slides.title: Edge AI for Predictive Maintenance
slides.subtitle: Catching failures before they happen — on the machine, not in the cloud

slides.title: Why the cloud round-trip is too slow
The cost of unplanned downtime
bullets: A single line stoppage can cost $20K–$260K per hour
Reactive maintenance is 3–5x more expensive than planned work
Cloud inference adds 200–800ms of latency per reading
Bandwidth costs scale with every sensor you add
Connectivity gaps on the plant floor mean missed alerts
Vibration anomalies can develop in seconds, not minutes

slides.title: What inference-at-the-edge buys you
slides.caption: reduction in unplanned downtime when anomaly detection runs locally on the equipment

left.heading: Cloud-only
bullets: Single point of failure on outage

slides.title: Anatomy of an edge node
slides.body: A microcontroller-class NPU samples accelerometer and current sensors, runs a quantized anomaly model in a fixed memory budget, and emits a health score every cycle. The gateway aggregates scores and forwards only flagged events.

slides.quote: We stopped shipping every byte to the cloud and started shipping decisions. The factory got quieter — and so did the pager.
